'''Ship''' was the name of the [[Sith Meditation Sphere]] found by [[Ben Skywalker]] in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]]. He was an ancient being whose purpose was to train [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]; to that end, he reached out to the [[Lost Tribe of Sith]] on [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]] and imparted knowledge of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large to the isolated Tribe, bringing them off-world and guiding them to build up a [[Sith armada (Lost Tribe of the Sith)|Sith armada]] to destroy the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. Ship obeyed masters of the dark side who had a strong will, and communicated thoughts with organic beings via [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]; his first contact among the Lost Tribe was the Sith [[Vestara Khai]]. However, as the Tribe amassed their forces, Ship was forced to submit to the will of a far stronger being—the dark side entity [[Abeloth]]. Commanded by Abeloth, Ship led a [[Lost Tribe of Sith strike team|strike team]] of the Tribe to [[Abeloth's planet]], triggering the series of events in the [[Lost Tribe of Sith emergence]]. The Jedi and Sith ultimately defeated Abeloth, and Ship helped Vestara Khai to flee the Jedi in search of other Sith in the galaxy.

==Description==
A [[Gender/Legends|male]]-programmed [[Sith Meditation Sphere]], Ship had a round cockpit and resembled [[Yuuzhan Vong]] creations, but was inorganic and had a rusty red-orange [[Color/Legends|color]]. Its main weapon shot large spheres at a target. The interior was bare. The pilot sat in a meditative posture in the center of the cockpit and communicated mentally with Ship.

==History==
[[File:Benfury.jpg|thumb|left|150px|[[Ben Skywalker]], who found Ship on [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]]]]
Ship was first used by Ben Skywalker in [[40 ABY/Legends|40 ABY]] after Ben found him on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]]. Ben flew Ship to the [[Anakin Solo (starship)|''Anakin Solo'']], where he presented it to [[Darth Caedus|Jacen Solo]], who then gave it to [[Lumiya]]. Ship surprised Lumiya by saying it had found her. Lumiya was using Ship when she was followed by [[Mara Jade Skywalker]] to [[Hesperidium/Legends|Hesperidium]]. The two former [[Emperor's Hand/Legends|Emperor's Hands]] [[Duel on Hesperidium|dueled]], and if not for Ship's interference, Lumiya's survival would have been questionable. Ship intervened because its purpose was to train apprentices to fight, and it disliked the idea of its "students" dying. For this reason, Ship allowed Skywalker and Lumiya to fight, but would not let either one be killed.

Later, Lumiya used Ship to follow Jacen to the [[Hapes Consortium/Legends|Hapes Consortium]], where she discovered that he had a [[Tenel Ka Djo|lover]] and [[Allana Djo Solo|child]]. Although Lumiya and Ship helped Jacen by firing at the pursuing [[StealthX]] of Mara Jade Skywalker, they were assaulted by Jacen, who wished to keep his relationship with Tenel Ka secret.

[[File:Alema_Rarheadshot.jpg|thumb|right|175px|[[Alema Rar]], who took a special interest in Ship]]
Following Lumiya's death at the hands of [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], Alema Rar took control of Ship, held at the [[Roqoo Depot]] by the Hapans. Her first action after assuming command was to take Ship to the [[Jedi Academy (Ossus)|Jedi Academy]] on [[Ossus/Legends|Ossus]], then to [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], where she met with the [[One Sith]].

Following her trip to Korriban, Alema Rar traveled to [[Kashyyyk/Legends|Kashyyyk]], where she used Ship to save [[Darth Caedus]]'s life during the ensuing [[Battle of Kashyyyk (Second Galactic Civil War)|battle]].

Later, Ship took Alema to Lumiya's asteroid where the pair came into conflict with [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Zekk]] and [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]]. During the fight Zekk tapped into old [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|Dark side]] training from the [[Shadow Academy]] and persuaded Ship to seek a more suitable master. Ship fled from [[the Home]] and was pursued by the [[Poison Moon (starship)|''Poison Moon'']] to Ziost. [[Dician]], the commander of the ''Poison Moon'', attempted to convince Ship to join the One Sith, but she was turned down. Ship didn't think that it was wise for her cult to remain hidden and instead sought out others who would be more compelled to take what was theirs.

Later in [[41 ABY]], Ship arrived on the planet [[Kesh/Legends|Kesh]], where he encountered a [[Lost Tribe of Sith]]; namely a young [[Human/Legends|human]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] named [[Vestara Khai]]. Ship sensed Khai's desire to be a [[Sith Master/Legends|Sith Master]] and offered to teach her in the ways of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. Over the next two years, Ship helped the Tribe to build a small [[Sith armada (Lost Tribe of the Sith)|Sith armada]], by assisting the Sith attack and capture other ships they encountered in space. However, in [[43 ABY|43.5 ABY]] Ship left Kesh abruptly, much to the dismay of the [[Circle of Lords]], who planned to recapture it.<ref name="Omen">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Omen]]''</ref>

The team of Sith followed Ship to a [[Abeloth's planet|planet]] controlled by a being called [[Abeloth]]. Abeloth had called Ship to her and now had complete power over it. After losing many of their members due to meddling by Abeloth, the strike team left the planet, without Ship.<ref name="Abyss" />

Later, Luke and [[Sarasu Taalon|High Lord Taalon's]] [[Lost Tribe of Sith|Sith strike force]] returned to [[Maw Cluster/Legends|the Maw]] to confront Abeloth. [[Jaina Solo Fel|Jaina Solo]] accompanied Luke to destroy Ship in her [[StealthX]]. Under Abeloth's control Ship attacked the StealthX until it was severely damaged, and then abruptly left the planet for space. This coincided with the moment Abeloth was subdued and lost control over both Ship and the paranoid Jedi.<ref name="Allies">''[[Fate of the Jedi: Allies]]''</ref>

However, Abeloth was not dead as she had lead others to believe and had masqueraded as the now deceased [[Dyon Stadd]] to fool the Jedi and Sith. Despite being weakened by the Jedi and the loss of one of her host avatars, Abeloth regained her control over Ship and used him to draw the Sith and Jedi to the [[Pool of Knowledge]] to distract them while she stole the ''[[Jade Shadow]]'' and escaped her planet.

Ship remained enslaved to Abeloth during such times as her battles with the Jedi on [[Nam Chorios/Legends|Nam Chorios]], her alliance with the Lost Tribe of Sith, and her ruling of both the Tribe and the Galactic Alliance. During the [[Liberation of Coruscant (Lost Tribe of Sith emergence)|Liberation of Coruscant]], Abeloth fled on Ship with a captive Vestara Khai and Ben Skywalker. When Abeloth was killed by Ben and Vestara, and her remaining avatars were destroyed, Ship was finally released from her hold and rushed to save Vestara from the Jedi whom she had betrayed back on Coruscant. There, he named her a [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lady]] and told her of the existence of [[One Sith|another faction of Sith]] in the galaxy that would need her experience with the Skywalkers and his knowledge of Sith training to develop. The two then left to join this new order.

==Behind the scenes==
Although Ship is italicized in its entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', it is not italicized in the novels—including novels published after ''The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia''—in which it has appeared. This article does not italicize the name due to the majority of sources not doing so.
